channel.title: +Articles on आगमे वैष्णवे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on आगमे वैष्णवे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः

item.title: Mukta bodha intro
item.description:
Roots

Contained within the Mahābhārata, the Nārāyaṇīya teaches that the divine reality is comprised of a processual formation (vyūha) that, taken as a whole, is Viṣṇu-Nārāyaṇa himself. Each facet of the divine person (Nārāyaṇa, Vāsudeva, Saṃkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha) corresponds to a succession of states within each human being (thinking, feeling, willing, knowing, acting) and also to the process whereby a person loses track of, and then recovers, an awareness of their true nature.

item.title: अध्य्-एकायनम्
item.description:
अभिप्रायान्तरम्

एकायनशाखा कठस्यैवोपशाखासीद् इत्यनिमेषः - द्वयोर् अपि काश्मीरमूलत्वात्।
मानसतरङ्गिणीकृतो ऽभिप्रायः पृथग् रक्षितः।

Source: RavilochanaH

There is no record of an ekayana shakha in sukla yajur Veda. Rather pAncharAtra texts talk abt kanva shakha only. Otoh a pancaratra sruti and pancaratra upanishad are quoted by kashmira shaiva acharyas like utpala. My presumption is that just as taittiriya shakha has vaikhanasa branch with its own mantrapatha, pancaratrins must have been of kanva shakha with some mantrapatha/sruti of their own.
